item.title: U.S. sinks 6 Iranian boats as Iran attacks UAE, ship in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran launches a new attack on the UAE and a tanker in the Strait of Hormuz as the U.S. military says 6 Iranian small boats were destroyed in a naval clash.

item.title: Want to borrow home equity without refinancing? 3 options to consider this May
item.description: Want to borrow home equity without having to give up your low mortgage rate? Here are three ways to do so this May.

item.title: Deadly hantavirus outbreak reported on cruise ship, officials say
item.description: Several deaths and sicknesses on a cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean are being blamed on the rare hantavirus infection. CBS News' Ramy Inocencio reports.

item.title: Britney Spears pleads guilty to reckless driving in DUI case, sentenced to 12 months probation
item.description: Britney Spears pleaded guilty to reckless driving on Monday, avoiding jail time following her March arrest.

item.title: How the Spirit Airlines shutdown impacts budget-conscious travelers
item.description: Nathan Bomey, a business reporter for Axios, joins CBS News 24/7 to break down how Spirit Airlines' closure impacts budget-conscious travelers.

item.title: Apparent hantavirus outbreak kills 3 on cruise ship, sickens at least 3 more
item.description: A suspected outbreak of the rare hantavirus on a cruise ship in the Atlantic off Africa killed three people and sickened at least three others, health officials say.

item.title: Correspondents' dinner shooting suspect moved off suicide watch ahead of court appearance
item.description: Cole Allen, the suspected shooter at the White House Correspondents' Dinner, was moved off suicide watch in jail ahead of his appearance in court on Monday. CBS News senior coordinating producer Anna Schecter has more.

item.title: Two U.S. merchant vessels successfully transit Strait of Hormuz, CENTCOM says
item.description: Two U.S. merchant vessels successfully transited through the Strait of Hormuz, CENTCOM said, after President Trump announced a plan called "Project Freedom," where the U.S. will guide ships through the chokepoint. CBS News contributor Aaron MacLean and Senior White House correspondent Nancy Cordes have more.

item.title: Supreme Court temporarily restores access to abortion pill mifepristone via teleheath and mail
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Samuel Alito temporarily restored access to the abortion pill mifepristone by telehealth and mail after an appellate court order blocked an FDA rule on Friday. CBS News contributor Jessica Levinson has more.

item.title: 89-year-old Kentucky Derby fan gets special wish granted
item.description: Bob Weihe was able to attend his 80th consecutive Kentucky Derby over the weekend and witness a historic race.

item.title: 2 dead after driver plows into people in Leipzig, Germany
item.description: Police said several were injured in the incident, which happened in the Grimmaische Strasse, a street that leads into central Leipzig's shopping area.

item.title: What are today's mortgage and mortgage refinance interest rates?
item.description: Knowing today's mortgage rates can help new homebuyers and homeowners looking to lock in a good deal.

item.title: Is now a good time to take out a personal loan for debt consolidation?
item.description: Personal loan costs may be lower than credit cards right now, but that's not the only factor you should weigh.

item.title: Derby-winning trainer hopes more women will succeed after historic win
item.description: Cherie DeVaux reflected on her place in history after becoming the first woman to train a Kentucky Derby winner with Golden Tempo.

item.title: Rudy Giuliani hospitalized in critical condition, spokesperson says
item.description: The 81-year-old former New York City mayor has been hospitalized for pneumonia, his spokesperson said.

item.title: Mysterious world beyond Pluto may have atmosphere, study says
item.description: If confirmed, the rock would become just the second world past Neptune in our solar system to host an atmosphere.

item.title: What does Spirit Airlines' shutdown mean for travelers?
item.description: Have tickets to fly on Spirit? Here's what to know about refunds and alternative flights now that the budget airline has ceased operations.

item.title: Why the Spirit Airlines federal bailout didn't happen
item.description: Alison Sider, a reporter for The Wall Street Journal, joins CBS News 24/7 to share details on why the Spirit Airlines bailout deal didn't go through ahead of the airline shuttering early Saturday morning.

item.title: Supreme Court temporarily halts mail ban on abortion pill mifepristone
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Samuel Alito temporarily restored the FDA rule that allows the abortion pill mifepristone to be prescribed online and sent by mail after a Louisiana appellate court blocked the 2023 rule on Friday. CBS News' Katrina Kaufman has the latest.

item.title: How to know when credit card debt forgiveness is the wrong call (and what to do instead)
item.description: Debt forgiveness can be a smart strategy to use, but for some borrowers, it creates more problems than it solves.

item.title: Cherie DeVaux on making history with Golden Tempo's Kentucky Derby win
item.description: Cherie DeVaux, who became the first woman to train a Kentucky Derby winner after Golden Tempo shocked the field on Saturday, joins CBS News 24/7 to discuss making history and what's next for the colt.

item.title: Video shows United flight strike truck on N.J. Turnpike before landing at Newark
item.description: An investigation is underway after a United Airlines plane struck a light pole and truck on the New Jersey Turnpike as it was coming in for a landing at Newark Liberty Airport on Sunday afternoon, officials said.

item.title: Spirit Airlines says it has refunded most customers after shutdown
item.description: Some Spirit Airlines customers with flight tickets could be out of luck after the discount carrier ceased operations.

item.title: Lizzo opens up about new music amid yearslong legal battle
item.description: In an exclusive interview with "CBS Mornings" co-host Gayle King, Lizzo spoke about releasing new music and her ongoing legal battle, saying, "the truth is less salacious than the headlines."

item.title: Alito temporarily restores FDA rule allowing abortion pill to be sent by mail
item.description: A federal appeals court blocked a FDA rule that allowed the abortion pill mifepristone to be dispensed through the mail.

item.title: GameStop offers to buy eBay for $55.5 billion, threatens hostile bid
item.description: EBay said on Monday that it will "carefully review" GameStop's unsolicited $ 125-per-share takeover offer.

item.title: All the past Met Gala themes over the years
item.description: The 2026 Met Gala dress code is "Fashion is Art" — styled to go along with the spring exhibition theme "Costume Art."

item.title: Record cocaine haul seized in Atlantic by Spanish police, union says
item.description: Spanish police impounded what is believed to be a national record haul of cocaine from a ship, with the seizure potentially reaching 40 tons, a union said.

item.title: A tool to help keep dementia in check
item.description: Many people fear that a family history of dementia dooms them to inevitably suffer the condition themselves. But a new tool, the Brain Care Score, shows how lifestyle changes can be beneficial, cutting the risk of dementia.

item.title: Drugmaker asks Supreme Court to halt ruling blocking online access to mifepristone
item.description: A maker of the widely used abortion pill mifepristone​ asked the Supreme Court on Saturday to block an appellate court ruling that cut off mail-order access to the drug just a day earlier.
